novel_features.name: File-format filter
novel_features.description: Narrow craft results to a specific cut/print file format (SVG, DXF, PNG, EPS, PES) that Creative Fabrica has no facet for.
novel_features.rationale: File format is not an Algolia facet; it only exists inside tags and titles, so it requires local full-text filtering over the synced catalog.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this when a crafter needs a machine-ready format the web UI can't filter on.
novel_features.group: Filters the web UI doesn't have

novel_features.name: Real-discount ranking
novel_features.description: Rank on-sale items by their actual regular-to-sale price drop, not just whether they are flagged on sale.
novel_features.rationale: The API only filters on a promotions flag; ranking by true discount depth requires computing the delta per item locally.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this to surface the deepest genuine discounts instead of discount-theater.

novel_features.name: Designer profile stats
novel_features.description: Profile a designer's catalog: product-type mix, price band, free count, POD count, and newest release.
novel_features.rationale: Requires aggregating across a designer's full synced catalog, which no single Creative Fabrica API call returns.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Pick this when you need a one-shot read on a creator instead of scrolling their page.

novel_features.name: Designer comparison
novel_features.description: Compare two designers head-to-head: catalog size, type mix, price band, free/POD share, and popularity.
novel_features.rationale: Requires a cross-designer join over two fetched catalogs that has no API or web equivalent.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this for competitor research when sourcing or scouting creators.

novel_features.name: New-since-snapshot diff
novel_features.description: Show only catalog items added since your last sync for a tracked query or designer.
novel_features.rationale: There is no 'what's new' API; it requires diffing object IDs against a prior local snapshot.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this to track fresh drops without re-scanning the whole catalog.

novel_features.name: Subscription-free filter
novel_features.description: Keep only assets usable outside an active subscription.
novel_features.rationale: The outsideSubscription attribute is returned per item but is not a server-side facet, so filtering on it requires a local post-filter.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this when you need standalone-licensable assets, distinct from free or POD.
novel_features.group: Filters the web UI doesn't have

novel_features.name: Tag and facet explorer
novel_features.description: Show the top tags and categories that co-occur with a query, to refine the next search.
novel_features.rationale: Requires rolling up tag and category frequency across the result set in the local store.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this to discover better refinement terms before a deep search.

narrative.headline: Search Creative Fabrica's 20M+ font, graphic, craft, and POD catalog from your terminal — with file-format and commercial-license filters the web UI lacks, plus designer tracking.
narrative.value_prop: Creative Fabrica's web UI is heavy, upsell-laden, and gives you only the facets it wants to. This CLI talks straight to the same catalog index the site uses, adds the filters crafters and print-on-demand sellers actually need: file format (svg/dxf/png/eps), commercial-license (POD), subscription-free, and real discount depth. It also tracks designers and surfaces what is new since your last run — all agent-native with --json, --csv, and --select.

quickstart.comment: Confirm the CLI is wired and the catalog index is reachable.
Search the catalog the way the website does.
Find commercial-license SVG cut files — a filter the web UI can't express.
Profile a designer's whole catalog at a glance.

troubleshoots.symptom: Search returns zero results for everything
troubleshoots.fix: Run 'creativefabrica-pp-cli doctor' — the public search key may have rotated; the CLI re-discovers it automatically, or set CREATIVEFABRICA_ALGOLIA_API_KEY / CREATIVEFABRICA_ALGOLIA_APP_ID to override.
The catalog key is referer-restricted; the CLI sends the required Origin/Referer headers automatically — upgrade if you see this on an old build.
Format is matched against tags and titles, not a server facet; broaden the query or try a sibling format (png vs svg).

narrative.when_to_use: Use this CLI when an agent or script needs to search, filter, or profile the Creative Fabrica design-asset catalog — finding fonts, graphics, SVG/DXF cut files, embroidery files, or POD-licensable designs by keyword, type, category, format, price, or designer. It is ideal for print-on-demand sourcing, Cricut/Silhouette project hunting, and tracking specific designers' new releases over time via the local mirror.
anti_triggers: Do not use this CLI to download, purchase, or access files in a user's personal Creative Fabrica library or favorites — v1 is the anonymous public catalog only.
Do not use this CLI to upload or sell products as a designer.
Do not use this CLI for Creative Fabrica's AI Studio generation tools.
Do not use this CLI to redeem the curated 24-hour daily-gifts — it surfaces the permanent free-item catalog, not the daily timed freebies.

recipes.title: Commercial-license cut files
recipes.explanation: POD-safe SVG candidates exported as CSV for a sourcing sheet.

recipes.title: Newest free fonts
recipes.explanation: The latest genuinely-free fonts in machine-readable form.

recipes.title: Deepest real discounts
recipes.explanation: On-sale items ranked by actual discount depth, narrowed to the price fields.

recipes.title: Track a designer's drops
recipes.explanation: Only the items this designer added since your last sync.

recipes.title: Refine a broad search
recipes.explanation: Top co-occurring tags to sharpen a vague query before a deep search.

trigger_phrases: find POD fonts on creative fabrica
